title: Julian Nagelsmann’s Official Germany Squad For The 2026 FIFA World Cup
original_text:
The Germany national team officially confirmed the players who will represent the country at the 2026 FIFA World Cup , where Die Mannschaft will look to regain its place among football’s elite after several disappointing tournaments.

The biggest story of the squad announcement is the return of Manuel Neuer , who will play another World Cup at 40 years old after months of uncertainty surrounding his international future.

The team managed by Julian Nagelsmann heads into the tournament in the United States, Mexico, and Canada with a combination of experience, youth, and outstanding attacking talent that once again makes Germany one of the most dangerous teams in the competition.

Even with Germany arriving full of elite young talent, all eyes will be on Manuel Neuer . The legendary goalkeeper returns to the national team after Julian Nagelsmann decided to trust his experience once again.

The Bayern Munich captain is expected to reclaim the starting role despite the strong performances Oliver Baumann delivered during qualifying. Now, Neuer will attempt to guide Germany deep into the tournament in what could become the final major international competition of his iconic career.

Beyond Neuer, Germany will count on two of the most talented footballers in the world in Florian Wirtz and Jamal Musiala . Both arrive as the main attacking hopes of a national team dreaming of competing for another world title.

Musiala, in particular, enters the tournament surrounded by some physical concerns after missing a large part of the season through injury. However, Nagelsmann made it clear that even below full fitness, he still considers the Bayern midfielder one of the best players on the planet.

One of the most surprising names in the squad is Lennart Karl , the young Bayern Munich attacking midfielder who appears as one of the major new faces in Germany’s World Cup roster.

The inclusion of Nadiem Amiri also caught attention after the Mainz midfielder earned his place thanks to an excellent Bundesliga campaign.

After several disappointing international tournaments and early eliminations, Germany heads into the 2026 World Cup under pressure to once again compete seriously for the championship.

With stars such as Neuer, Kimmich, Rüdiger, Musiala, and Wirtz, Die Mannschaft possesses one of the most talented squads in the tournament and hopes to restore the country to the top level of world football.

Follow all the action from the 2026 FIFA World Cup on beIN SPORTS.
